title: Gently Smoked Vietnamese Pork Bun Thit Nuong
description: A traditional Vietnamese dish featuring grilled pork, white cabbage, and a soft bun, all steamed to perfection in a bamboo steamer. This recipe is kid-friendly and halal-friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine yeast, flour, salt, and sugar, then gradually add in water to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a separate bowl, whisk together fish sauce, lime juice, and vegetable oil to make the marinade.
Add the sliced pork to the marinade and let it sit for at least 30 minutes.
Grill the pork for 5 minutes per side, or until it is cooked through.
Assemble the buns by placing a few slices of pork, some shredded cabbage, and a sprinkle of salt onto each bun.
Place the buns in a bamboo steamer and steam for 10 minutes, or until the buns are soft and the filling is heated through.
